Home | GitHub Source | Contact Me | Support the Site |

Main Series Home

Battle Subway Pokémon

The following Pokémon are used in the Battle Subway. Every individual species used by these facilities can have between one and six movesets associated with them. There are roughly four groups of 'power levels' of species that can be encountered:

  1. Low Tier: Consists largely of first-stage Pokémon and some weak evolved Pokémon. Species in this tier have two sets associated with them.
  2. Mid Tier: Consists largely of middle-stage Pokémon and some weak fully evolved Pokémon. Species in this tier have two sets associated with them.
  3. High Tier: Consists largely of fully-evolved Pokémon. Species in this tier have four sets associated with them.
  4. Legendary Tier: Consists of all Legendaries that are legal in the Battle Subway. Species in this tier have four sets associated with them.

The common way of referring to a Pokémon with multiple sets is to identify the set with a number, e.g. "Set2 Rayquaza", "Rayquaza1", or "Rayquaza-3". In some early documentation, sets may instead be referred to with the number given in the below table ("999 Rayquaza"). For my tables, I will be using the "Rayquaza-1" method of referring to sets.

  • Low Tier Set-1
  • Low Tier Set-2
  • Mid Tier Set-1
  • Mid Tier Set-2
  • High Tier Set-1
  • High Tier Set-2
  • High Tier Set-3
  • High Tier Set-4
  • Legendary Tier

Some additional resources:

  • This page contains a damage calculator with all the movesets of Battle Subway Pokémon pre-loaded, using the numbers for each set. Remember that you will need to update the IVs yourself in accordance with the Trainer faced.
  • This post contains some Speed values associated with enemy Pokémon with 31 IVs. This can be useful for determining what kinds of Speed benchmarks your Pokémon will need to hit to accomplish their goals.
  • The lookup tool linked here as "Battle Subway" allows you to enter a Trainer's name and their Pokémon and get information about the possible Pokémon you might face or are facing.
